summary: DHH responds to a Java programmer's article that characterized Rails deployment as primitive and barbaric compared to Java's established practices. Rather than firing back himself, he deliberately steps back and lets others make the case — his colleague Jamis Buck, Dave Thomas, and James Duncan Davidson (creator of Ant). The post highlights that the criticism was based on ignorance rather than actual investigation of Rails deployment practices. DHH notes the irony that many prominent Java developers, including Ant's creator, had already adopted Rails.
key_points: A Java programmer assumed Rails deployment meant manually zipping files, revealing ignorance of actual Rails practices
DHH deliberately restrains his temper and lets respected community members respond instead
Dave Thomas criticizes using ignorance as a weapon, noting it belittles discussion and alienates communities
Multiple high-profile Java developers had already switched to Rails, undermining the 'civilized vs barbaric' framing
The creator of Ant himself was working professionally with Rails at the time
money_quotes: I'm glad I held my breath for a few hours. Otherwise, I would probably have had to post another picture of a flower tomorrow.
The civilized world (Java programmers) are compared to the barbaric horde (Rails programmers).
I really don't like the way that ignorance is used as a weapon. It belittles the discussion, reflects badly on the poster, and alienates communities that have a lot to learn from each other.
Why would you think that they would suddenly forget the importance of version control and sane deployment practices because they picked up a more agile environment?
key_insight: Criticizing a technology you haven't actually investigated reveals more about your own ignorance than about the technology's shortcomings.
